seoTitle: Hopkins Creek vs Horningsea Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hopkins Creek and Horningsea Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hopkins Creek vs Horningsea Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hopkins Creek and Horningsea Park.
heading: Hopkins Creek vs Horningsea Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hopkins Creek, New South Wales and Horningsea Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hopkins Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Horningsea Park (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hopkins Creek or Horningsea Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hopkins Creek has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Horningsea Park scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hopkins Creek scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Horningsea Park scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hopkins Creek or Horningsea Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hopkins Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
